message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-mesh: finish lane 2 zone-gate plumbing — PeerState.zones + opt-in enforcement

Previous lane 2 commit (a14273cd) left fcp-mesh failing to compile:
the new symbol-request zone check referenced `state.zones` on PeerState
without adding the field, and the new SymbolRequestError::UnauthorizedZone
variant was not covered in symbol_request_reason_code. This lands the
remaining pieces:

- PeerState gains `zones: HashSet<ZoneId>`, an attestation-driven
  authorization set populated by the transport layer. An empty set means
  "not yet populated" — the gate skips enforcement to preserve existing
  behavior for callers that have not wired attestation-driven zone
  membership. Tests constructing PeerState directly get HashSet::new().
- MeshNode::update_peer_zones(node_id, zones) setter for callers to
  opt into enforcement after verifying an attestation.
- MeshNode::update_peer_state preserves any previously stored zones
  for the same node id so a profile refresh does not silently downgrade
  an authorized peer to the "not populated" state.
- SymbolRequestError::UnauthorizedZone now maps to reason code
  "unauthorized_zone" in symbol_request_reason_code.
- mesh_integration + no_mock_integration callsites updated for the
  decode_control_plane / process_control_plane_frame signatures that
  now take peer and now_ms (the admission-controller plumbing from
  the same lane 2 commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
